scenes.text: You are a signal technician on a remote research station. Alarms blare — the communication tower has gone offline. Without it, the crew cannot call for rescue. You have two options.

choices.text: Climb the tower and fix it manually
Check the control room for a software fault

scenes.text: You climb the icy tower. Halfway up, a bolt snaps. You hang by one arm. You spot the broken antenna cable just above you.

choices.text: Push through the pain and reconnect the cable
Descend carefully and try another way

scenes.text: In the control room you find a corrupted firmware file. You have a backup drive but it is an older version. A colleague radios in — they can talk you through a manual patch.

choices.text: Apply the older backup and restart
Follow your colleague's instructions for the manual patch

scenes.text: The signal is restored. A rescue ship picks up your beacon within hours. The crew is safe. You are hailed as a hero.
The older firmware causes a cascade failure. The entire station loses power. The crew spends three days in the dark before a passing satellite detects your emergency beacon.
